title: Scoring and the model registry
text: cognitive-task scores LEVANTE IRT ability estimates (θ). produced fitting item-response models (calibration) scoring child’s trial data appropriate fitted model. fitted models bookkeeping needed find live levante_metadata_scoring dataset Redivis, rlevante reads handful functions: End end, scoring task flows like :
code: get_trials(data_source)             # raw trial data    |> recode_trials()               # make trials scoring-ready (see below)  fetch_scoring_table()               # look up the model spec for (task, dataset) fetch_registry_dir()                # handle for downloading the fitted model  score(task, dataset, trials, runs,  # -> downloads the ModelRecord, shapes the       scoring_table, registry_dir)  #    data, rebuilds the model, scores it

title: Scoring and the model registry
text: Every rlevante “get” function takes data_source version. reproducible analyses pin fully qualified reference form name:hash:version rather relying version = "current", silently tracks latest release. persistent hash (e.g. d0rt) stable even dataset renamed; trailing :v1_0 pins version. can find dataset’s Redivis page API Information → Qualified references (see “Reference identifiers” section README). call “get” function version = "current", rlevante emits message reporting qualified reference resolved , can copy pin future runs.
code: # tracks whatever the latest release happens to be scores_current <- get_scores("levante_data_example:d0rt", version = "current")  # pinned: name + persistent dataset id (hash) + dataset version scores_pinned <- get_scores("levante_data_example:d0rt:v1_0")

what: Trials must be recoded before scoring
title: Scoring and the model registry
text: get_trials() returns correct column, column yet scoring-ready. calibrated models fit data passed recode_trials() first, must apply scoring get subtly wrong numbers. recode_trials() performs several task-specific corrections, including: Slider items (e.g. number line): marks response correct falls within slider_threshold (default 0.15) target, sets chance level 1 / slider_threshold / 100 accordingly. Hearts & Flowers (hf): codes -fast (<200 ms) -slow (>2000 ms) responses incorrect labels trial start/stay/switch. /Different Selection (sds) Theory Mind (tom): recodes correctness disaggregates items. Known item-key fixes: e.g. math_subtract_37_24 rescored corrected answer.

title: Scoring and the model registry
text: Putting together, reproduce scores one task dataset compare published values. exported score() function high-level entry point — looks model spec, downloads fitted ModelRecord, shapes data, scores .
code: scoring_table <- fetch_scoring_table() registry_dir  <- fetch_registry_dir()  task <- "math" dataset <- "pilot_uniandes_co_bogota"  # trial (and, for adaptive tasks, run) data for this task + dataset trials_task <- get_trials("levante_data_example:d0rt") |>   recode_trials() |>   filter(item_task == task, dataset == !!dataset)  my_scores <- score(   task = task, dataset = dataset,   trials = trials_task, runs = NULL,   scoring_table = scoring_table, registry_dir = registry_dir )  # compare to the published scores published <- get_scores("levante_data_example:d0rt") |>   filter(task_id == task, dataset == !!dataset)  my_scores |>   inner_join(published, by = "run_id", suffix = c("_mine", "_pub")) |>   summarise(cor = cor(score_mine, score_pub))

title: Scoring and the model registry
text: score() downloads fitted model ModelRecord object. want inspect model directly — example look item parameters, validate rescore — can download one registry. accessors slots need: details exposed S4 slots: Two invariants worth knowing. First, mod_rec@data’s columns guaranteed items(mod_rec) order — scoring code must align new data order calling mirt::fscores(), matches columns position. Second, scores(mod_rec) holds EAP scores computed calibration time full data matrix, ground-truth reference can validate rescore :
code: # look up and download the ModelRecord for a (task, dataset) spec <- fetch_scoring_table() |>   filter(item_task == "math", dataset == "pilot_uniandes_co_bogota") |>   as.list() mod_rec <- get_registry_file(model_spec_filename(spec), fetch_registry_dir()) # rescoring the calibration runs should reproduce scores(mod_rec)$ability scores(mod_rec)

title: Scoring and the model registry
text: deployed IRT models include fixed guessing lower asymptote item’s chance level (g = chance, held fixed rather estimated). score_irt() rebuilds full model model_vals(mod_rec) — includes g rows — released ability scores already account guessing. Two things easy get wrong : item_parameters table (levante::get_item_parameters()) exposes difficulty discrimination; omits g. itemtype label (rasch / 2pl) describes discrimination structure , presence guessing. authoritative, complete parameter set model_vals(mod_rec) (.e. mirt::mod2values()), includes slope , intercept/difficulty d / b, guessing asymptote g, upper asymptote u. read item’s fixed guessing value: reconstruct item response function — person-fit, test-information, simulation, independent rescoring — must include asymptote: Reconstructing plain logistic without g silently mismatches fitted model inflates residuals low ability (lucky guesses hard items look “impossible”), can corrupt person-fit reliability analyses even though released scores correct. trial-level chance field get_trials() carries per-item g values, equivalent source asymptote.
code: model_vals(mod_rec) |>   filter(name == "g") |>   select(item, value)   # e.g. 0.25 for a 4-AFC task such as matrix P(theta) = g + (1 - g) * plogis(a * (theta - b))

title: LEVANTE Data Processing and Modeling
text: Internal R tooling LEVANTE data processing psychometric modeling. package holds model intermediates — trial recoding, IRT scoring pipeline, model registry, ModelRecord class. loading LEVANTE data R (user-facing get_* functions), see companion levante package. levantemodels internally-facing tooling depends levante data access. useful links: - overview LEVANTE project. - Details LEVANTE’s scoring psychometrics. - Details LEVANTE child tasks.

title: LEVANTE Data Processing and Modeling
code: # install.packages("devtools") # if you need the devtools package devtools::install_github("levante-framework/levantemodels")

title: LEVANTE Data Processing and Modeling
text: Trial recoding — recode_trials() makes loaded trial data scoring-ready (slider thresholding, Hearts & Flowers / SDS / ToM recodes, item-key fixes). IRT scoring pipeline — score() / score_irt(), model registry accessors fetch_scoring_table(), fetch_registry_table(), fetch_registry_dir(). ModelRecord class accessors (items(), model_vals(), model_class(), scores()). Processing — process_trials(), process_runs(), process_surveys(), process_participants(). See Scoring model registry vignette full trials → score pipeline.

title: ModelRecord class definition — ModelRecord-class
text: fitted LEVANTE IRT model metadata, stored model registry. Access contents items(), model_class(), model_vals(), scores(); see vignette("scoring--model-registry") details.

what: Recode trial correctness for scoring — recode_trials
title: Recode trial correctness for scoring — recode_trials
text: recode_trials() applies task-specific corrections make get_trials() output scoring-ready, must applied IRT scoring: calibrated models fit recoded trials, scoring raw get_trials() output yields subtly wrong values. Corrections include slider thresholding (response correct within slider_threshold target, chance set 1 / slider_threshold / 100), Hearts & Flowers RT start/stay/switch coding, /Different Selection Theory Mind recodes, known item-key fixes (e.g. math_subtract_37_24), chance-level backfill. See vignette("scoring--model-registry").
